articles.title: Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
articles.description: Lorde was performing at the Real Cool Festival in Madrid on Thursday and took some time during her set to speak out against AI glasses. While she didn't specify any brands in particular, it's likely she was taking a shot at festival sponsor Ray-Ban, which has collaborated with Meta on a pair of AI smartglasses. […]
articles.author: Terrence O’Brien

articles.title: Shall We Go On Sinning So That Grace May Increase? is hypnotic, healing, and hopeful
articles.description: Matmos are an incredibly accomplished duo between their own solo records like the masterpiece A Chance to Cut Is a Chance to Cure and production classic Björk records like Vespertine. But Drew Daniel, one half of Matmos, is fiendishly prolific. When he's not literally dreaming up new viral music genres, he's also putting out records […]
articles.author: Terrence O’Brien

articles.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
articles.description: Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details […]
articles.author: Terrence O’Brien

articles.title: One of SteelSeries' best gaming headsets is over $100 off
articles.description: SteelSeries has the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless gaming headset on sale for $239.99 (currently between $300 and $350 at other retailers). The Xbox version that supports a host of other platforms including PlayStation, PC, and Switch is available in both black and white. The PlayStation version, which supports the same platforms except for Xbox, is […]
articles.author: Brad Bourque

articles.title: How Philips Hue got the smart home right
articles.description: The state of the smart home can be frustrating, because it is just so obvious how things ought to work. You should be able to control everything from everywhere. Your spaces should adapt to what you're doing and how you're feeling. Making your home smart shouldn't require renovating, and the smarts should be mostly invisible. […]
articles.author: David Pierce

articles.title: Less is more with the Oura Ring 5
articles.description: If you're reading an Oura Ring 5 review at The Verge, you likely fall into one of two camps: newcomers looking for a smartwatch alternative, or Oura users pondering an upgrade. In the case of the former, this is a great casual health tracker and the best smart ring on the market - but not […]
articles.author: Victoria Song

articles.title: Blue Prince became a bonding — and learning — experience for my family
articles.description: I've always been the gamer in the family. When my son was born nearly 11 years ago, gaming was one of the things I looked forward to sharing with him. Pulling up a chair next to me, he would watch as I played Sea of Thieves with friends, often wearing the too-big-for-his-head headset to talk […]
articles.author: John.Higgins

articles.title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
articles.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the data center buildout, follow Emma Roth.
